scenario: A developer downloads Camunda 8 Run for local development. They open the unpacked c8run directory and want to start the bundled stack on Linux/macOS vs Windows.
question: Which Camunda 8 Run launcher commands are correct for each OS?

options.text: This answer contains fabricated launcher script names. exe is not the correct Windows launcher name. exe start. bat script would result in "command not found" or "file not found" errors.

8 distribution for any operating system -- not for Windows, Linux, or macOS. exe stop to stop). /c8run start. bat file would fail immediately because the file is absent from the unpacked distribution directory.
exe start (per :80). exe stop (per :597). exe binary directly with start/stop subcommands. exe start --docker).

Camunda 8 Run is specifically designed as a bundled developer quickstart that automatically orchestrates all required components with a single command. exe start command starts the entire bundled Orchestration Cluster including Zeebe gateway, Operate, Tasklist, Identity, and Connectors as a unified local stack. No separate commands per component are needed. This single-command startup is the primary value proposition of Camunda 8 Run for local development environments.

a.text: Incorrect. Fabricated start.bat does not exist in 8.8 distribution.

b.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 Run 8.8 launcher commands per c8run.md: Linux / macOS start (:65, :71): ./start.sh (Bash wrapper) OR ./c8run start (binary direct). Linux / macOS stop (:590): ./shutdown.sh. Windows start (:80): .\c8run.exe start (directly the Go binary). Windows stop (:597): .\c8run.exe stop. Docker mode: append --docker to start (e.g., ./start.sh --docker per :108, .\c8run.exe start --docker per :175). Custom configuration / database (:117-155, :182+): --config configuration/application-.yaml flag or ORCHESTRATION_CONFIG_FILE=... env var (Windows: set ORCHESTRATION_CONFIG_FILE=...). Port override: --port N CLI flag per c8run-troubleshooting.md:38-39. All commands orchestrate the bundled Orchestration Cluster (Zeebe + Operate + Tasklist + Identity) on a single consolidated port (default :8080 with path-prefix routing per component) plus management :9600 for Actuator endpoints. There is NO start.bat or shutdown.bat in the 8.8 c8run distribution -- the Windows-native flow uses the c8run.exe binary's start/stop subcommands.

c.text: Correct. No start.bat to use cross-OS.

d.text: Incorrect. c8run binary / start.sh orchestrates all components automatically.
